meta.title: SAFARI — convulsive seizure risk during aneurysmal SAH admission
meta.authors: Jaja BNR, Schweizer TA, Claassen J, et al.
meta.outcome: Risk of a convulsive seizure during hospital admission for aneurysmal subarachnoid haemorrhage (in patients on seizure prophylaxis), from 4 factors
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. The SAFARI score (Jaja et al., Neurosurgery 2018; SAHIT collaborators) stratifies the risk of a convulsive seizure during admission for aneurysmal subarachnoid haemorrhage. Developed on 1500 and validated on 852 patients (all on seizure prophylaxis).

How to read it

  • Score 0–5: Low 0–1 (3–5%), Moderate 2–3 (7–8%), High 4–5 (17–22%).
  • An onset seizure counts 2 points; age ≥60, hydrocephalus and anterior-circulation aneurysm count 1 each.

Predicts in-hospital acute convulsive seizures (not late epilepsy); risk %s are from the validation cohort. Demonstration tool.

predictors.name: Onset seizure (before/at hospitalisation)
predictors.assess: A seizure at the onset of the SAH or before hospital admission. 2 points (strongest factor, OR 5.75).

predictors.name: Hydrocephalus requiring CSF diversion
predictors.assess: Enlarged ventricles treated with an external ventricular drain or serial lumbar puncture. 1 point (OR 2.46).

model.panel_title: Convulsive seizure risk during SAH admission
model.note: In-hospital convulsive seizure risk (patients on prophylaxis). Risk %s from the validation cohort. Groups: Low 0–1 · Moderate 2–3 · High 4–5.

provenance.source: Jaja 2018 Neurosurgery Table 4 (points) + validation risk-per-score (text) — doi 10.1093/neuros/nyx334, PMID 28973169
provenance.notes: SAFARI 0-5: onset-seizure 2, age≥60 1, hydrocephalus 1, anterior-circulation 1. Validation risk: 0=3.2,1=4.8,2=7.0,3=7.6,4=16.7,5=22.2%. AUC dev 0.77/val 0.65. No logistic intercept → discrete lookup. Built 2026-07-08.
